name: Noun gender and agreement
description: Recognize masculine, feminine, and neuter nouns and make nearby adjectives and pronouns agree with them.

example.text: Nowy student ma nową książkę i nowe ćwiczenie.
example.translation: The new student has a new book and a new exercise.

name: Masculine consonant-stem nouns
description: Decline common masculine nouns ending in a consonant across the basic singular and plural cases.

example.text: Profesor rozmawia ze studentem o kursie.
example.translation: The professor is talking with the student about the course.

name: Masculine nouns ending in -a
description: Decline masculine personal nouns such as kolega and sprzedawca with largely feminine-looking endings.

example.text: Czekam na kolegę, który pracuje jako sprzedawca.
example.translation: I am waiting for a friend who works as a salesperson.

name: Feminine nouns ending in -a
description: Use the regular case endings of feminine nouns ending in -a.

example.text: Rozmawiam z siostrą o naszej nowej pracy.
example.translation: I am talking with my sister about our new job.

name: Feminine nouns ending in -i
description: Decline feminine personal nouns ending in -i, including pani and gospodyni.

example.text: Dziękuję pani za rozmowę z gospodynią.
example.translation: I thank you, ma'am, for the conversation with the hostess.

name: Neuter nouns ending in -o
description: Decline regular neuter nouns ending in -o and form their plurals.

example.text: To okno jest otwarte, a tamte okna są zamknięte.
example.translation: This window is open, and those windows are closed.

name: Neuter nouns ending in -e
description: Decline common neuter action and result nouns ending in -e.

example.text: To ćwiczenie nie ma trudnego pytania.
example.translation: This exercise does not have a difficult question.

name: Basic proper-name declension
description: Apply Polish case endings to familiar personal and geographical names.

example.text: Anna mieszka w Krakowie, ale pracuje w Warszawie.
example.translation: Anna lives in Kraków but works in Warsaw.

name: Common irregular nouns
description: Learn frequent nouns whose stems or endings change irregularly, such as człowiek, brat, rok, and dziecko.

example.text: Ten człowiek ma dwoje dzieci i jednego brata.
example.translation: This person has two children and one brother.

name: Hard-stem adjective agreement
description: Decline adjectives ending in -y so they agree in gender, number, and case.

example.text: Mały chłopiec niesie małą torbę.
example.translation: The small boy is carrying a small bag.

name: Soft-stem adjective agreement
description: Decline adjectives ending in -i and preserve their spelling alternations.

example.text: Długi film opowiada o polskiej historii.
example.translation: The long film tells a story about Polish history.

name: Personal pronoun cases
description: Use the common case forms of ja, ty, on, ona, ono, my, wy, and oni.

example.text: Ona mnie zna, a ja często jej pomagam.
example.translation: She knows me, and I often help her.

name: Demonstratives ten, ta, to
description: Make demonstratives agree with their noun in gender, number, and case.

example.text: Podaj mi tę książkę i ten zeszyt.
example.translation: Hand me this book and this notebook.

name: Possessive pronouns
description: Decline mój, twój, nasz, and wasz like adjectives to express possession.

example.text: Mój brat odwiedza naszą babcię.
example.translation: My brother is visiting our grandmother.

name: Interrogative pronouns
description: Ask for people, things, qualities, choices, ownership, and quantity with kto, co, jaki, który, czyj, and ile.

example.text: Którą książkę czytasz i czyja ona jest?
example.translation: Which book are you reading, and whose is it?

name: Ordinal numerals
description: Decline ordinal numbers like adjectives when stating order, dates, floors, and times.

example.text: Mieszkamy na drugim piętrze od pierwszego maja.
example.translation: We have lived on the second floor since the first of May.

name: The numeral jeden
description: Make jeden agree with its noun while also using it as the number one.

example.text: Mam jedną siostrę i jednego brata.
example.translation: I have one sister and one brother.

name: Cardinals two to five
description: Use the basic gender and case forms of dwa, dwie, trzy, cztery, and pięć.

example.text: Kupiliśmy dwie bułki, trzy jabłka i pięć pomidorów.
example.translation: We bought two rolls, three apples, and five tomatoes.

name: Present-tense conjugation
description: Conjugate common imperfective verbs for person and number in the present tense.

example.text: Codziennie czytam, piszesz i rozmawiamy po polsku.
example.translation: Every day I read, you write, and we speak Polish.

name: Present tense of być
description: Use jestem, jesteś, jest, jesteśmy, jesteście, and są to identify or describe people and things.

example.text: Jestem studentką, a mój brat jest lekarzem.
example.translation: I am a student, and my brother is a doctor.

name: Past-tense person and gender
description: Build past forms whose endings show person, number, and often the subject's gender.

example.text: Wczoraj oglądałam film, a potem poszłam spać.
example.translation: Yesterday I watched a film and then went to sleep.

name: Simple and compound future
description: Use perfective present forms for simple future and będę with an infinitive or past form for compound future.

example.text: Jutro przeczytam list i będę pisać odpowiedź.
example.translation: Tomorrow I will read the letter and will be writing a reply.

name: Basic imperfective and perfective pairs
description: Learn frequent aspect partners lexically and distinguish an ongoing action from a completed result.

example.text: Czytam książkę, ale jeszcze jej nie przeczytałem.
example.translation: I am reading the book, but I have not finished it yet.

name: Nominative subjects and predicates
description: Use the nominative for subjects and for basic identifying predicates with to.

example.text: Ewa to nauczycielka, a Marek to jej uczeń.
example.translation: Ewa is a teacher, and Marek is her student.

name: Genitive possession and quantity
description: Use the genitive to relate nouns and after expressions of measure or quantity.

example.text: Na stole stoi butelka wody mojego kolegi.
example.translation: A bottle of my friend's water is on the table.

name: Genitive after verbal negation
description: Change many accusative direct objects to genitive when the verb is negated.

example.text: Mam czas, ale dziś nie mam czasu.
example.translation: I have time, but today I do not have time.

name: Accusative direct objects
description: Use the accusative for direct objects and learn its feminine and animate-masculine forms.

example.text: Mam kawę i widzę mojego kolegę.
example.translation: I have coffee and I see my friend.

name: Instrumental predicates and means
description: Use the instrumental after być for professions and roles, and for a means of transport or action.

example.text: Ona jest lekarką i jeździ do pracy tramwajem.
example.translation: She is a doctor and travels to work by tram.

name: Instrumental after z and location prepositions
description: Use the instrumental after z meaning with and after spatial prepositions such as przed, za, and nad.

example.text: Spotykam się z przyjaciółmi przed teatrem.
example.translation: I am meeting my friends in front of the theater.

name: Locative with w, na, po, and o
description: Use the locative after common prepositions for place, movement within an area, time, and topic.

example.text: Rozmawiamy o kursie w kawiarni na rynku.
example.translation: We are talking about the course in a café in the market square.

name: Basic questions and negated clauses
description: Form yes-no questions with czy, content questions with question words, and clauses negated by nie.

example.text: Czy Marta pracuje? Dlaczego nie ma jej w biurze?
example.translation: Is Marta working? Why isn't she in the office?

name: Basic compound and complex clauses
description: Connect clauses with i, a, lub, więc, ale, że, bo, dlatego że, and kiedy.

example.text: Zostałem w domu, bo padało, ale wieczorem wyszedłem.
example.translation: I stayed home because it was raining, but I went out in the evening.

name: Formal and informal register
description: Choose basic greetings, address forms, and pronouns that fit a formal or informal relationship.

example.text: Cześć, Aniu! Dzień dobry, pani doktor!
example.translation: Hi, Ania! Good morning, doctor!

name: Masculine animacy in the accusative
description: Distinguish animate masculine accusatives, which often match the genitive, from inanimate ones.

example.text: Widzę psa i stół, ale szukam mojego kota.
example.translation: I see a dog and a table, but I am looking for my cat.

name: Virile and non-virile plural
description: Use masculine-personal plural forms separately from other plural genders.

example.text: Studenci byli zmęczeni, a studentki były głodne.
example.translation: The male students were tired, and the female students were hungry.

name: Reflexive possessive swój
description: Use swój when the possessor is the subject of the clause.

example.text: Anna odwiedza swoją siostrę, a Piotr odwiedza swoją.
example.translation: Anna visits her sister, and Piotr visits his own.

name: Case forms of cardinal numerals
description: Decline cardinal numerals and distinguish forms such as dwaj, dwóch, and pięciu.

example.text: Rozmawiałem z dwoma studentami o pięciu projektach.
example.translation: I spoke with two students about five projects.

name: Imperative mood
description: Form direct commands and requests in the singular and plural.

example.text: Przeczytaj wiadomość i napiszcie odpowiedź.
example.translation: Read the message and write a reply.

name: Third-person commands with niech
description: Use niech with a present-tense form to express third-person commands and wishes.

example.text: Niech dzieci poczekają przed szkołą.
example.translation: Let the children wait in front of the school.

name: Conditional mood with by
description: Build conditional forms with a past stem and particles such as bym, byś, and byśmy.

example.text: Chciałbym zamówić kawę, a siostra wybrałaby herbatę.
example.translation: I would like to order coffee, and my sister would choose tea.

name: Comparative adjectives
description: Form regular, irregular, and analytic comparative adjectives, often followed by niż.

example.text: Pociąg jest szybszy niż autobus, ale bardziej zatłoczony.
example.translation: The train is faster than the bus but more crowded.

name: Comparative adverbs
description: Form regular, irregular, and analytic comparative adverbs.

example.text: Marta mówi szybciej, ale Piotr mówi bardziej wyraźnie.
example.translation: Marta speaks faster, but Piotr speaks more clearly.

name: Dative recipients and experiencers
description: Use the dative for recipients and people who experience states or reactions.

example.text: Dałem siostrze kwiaty, bo było jej smutno.
example.translation: I gave my sister flowers because she was sad.

name: Vocative forms of address
description: Use the vocative when directly addressing people in speech and correspondence.

example.text: Panie profesorze, drogi Marku, proszę zaczekać.
example.translation: Professor, dear Marek, please wait.

name: Basic genitive verb government
description: Learn frequent verbs that select a genitive complement, such as szukać and potrzebować.

example.text: Szukamy większego mieszkania i potrzebujemy pomocy.
example.translation: We are looking for a larger apartment and need help.

name: Case expressions of time and duration
description: Use case forms without prepositions to state dates, clock time, duration, and frequency.

example.text: W piątek pracowałem osiem godzin, a w sobotę tylko godzinę.
example.translation: On Friday I worked eight hours, and on Saturday only one hour.

name: Motion versus location with prepositions
description: Contrast motion toward a place with location there by selecting the required case.

example.text: Idę na pocztę, a potem będę na poczcie.
example.translation: I am going to the post office, and then I will be at the post office.

name: Meaning changes across preposition cases
description: Notice how a preposition's meaning changes with accusative, instrumental, genitive, or locative.

example.text: Kot wskoczył pod stół i teraz siedzi pod stołem.
example.translation: The cat jumped under the table and is now sitting under it.

name: Purpose clauses with żeby
description: Use żeby to state a purpose or requested outcome.

example.text: Przyszedłem wcześniej, żeby spokojnie porozmawiać.
example.translation: I came earlier so that we could talk calmly.

name: Expanded coordination
description: Connect alternatives, results, and contrasts with a także, albo, czy, dlatego, and ale.

example.text: Możemy zostać w domu albo pójść do kina, ale musimy zdecydować.
example.translation: We can stay home or go to the cinema, but we must decide.

name: Formal requests and correspondence
description: Combine formal address, polite conditionals, and conventional openings in short messages.

example.text: Szanowna Pani, czy mogłaby Pani przesłać formularz?
example.translation: Dear Madam, could you send the form?

name: Feminine consonant-stem nouns
description: Decline feminine nouns ending in a consonant, including abstract nouns and body parts.

example.text: Ta wiadomość wzbudziła radość, ale też niepewność.
example.translation: This news caused joy but also uncertainty.

name: Neuter nouns ending in -um
description: Keep -um nouns unchanged in the singular and decline them in the plural.

example.text: To muzeum współpracuje z kilkoma muzeami.
example.translation: This museum cooperates with several museums.

name: Neuter nouns ending in -ę
description: Handle stem expansion in neuter nouns such as imię, ramię, and zwierzę.

example.text: Nie pamiętam imienia dziecka z bolącym ramieniem.
example.translation: I do not remember the name of the child with the aching shoulder.

name: Masculine nouns ending in -anin
description: Decline nationality, origin, and affiliation nouns ending in -anin with their special plural stems.

example.text: Ten Amerykanin rozmawia z dwoma krakowianami.
example.translation: This American is talking with two residents of Kraków.

name: Indefinite pronouns
description: Use ktoś, coś, jakiś, and related forms for unknown or unspecified referents.

example.text: Ktoś zostawił coś ważnego na moim biurku.
example.translation: Someone left something important on my desk.

name: Negative pronouns and double negation
description: Combine nikt, nic, nigdy, and similar words with a negated verb.

example.text: Nikt niczego nie powiedział i nigdy tego nie wyjaśnił.
example.translation: Nobody said anything and never explained it.

name: Reflexive pronoun siebie and się
description: Use the case forms of the reflexive pronoun when subject and complement refer to the same participant.

example.text: Musisz o siebie dbać i wierzyć w siebie.
example.translation: You must take care of yourself and believe in yourself.

name: Virile forms of complex numerals
description: Select masculine-personal numeral forms and their governed noun cases.

example.text: W spotkaniu uczestniczyło dwudziestu czterech delegatów.
example.translation: Twenty-four delegates participated in the meeting.

name: Forming aspect pairs
description: Recognize common suffix and prefix patterns that relate imperfective and perfective verbs.

example.text: Codziennie zapisuję wyniki, a dziś zapisałem już wszystkie.
example.translation: I record the results every day, and today I have recorded them all.

name: Aspect for process, repetition, and result
description: Choose aspect according to repetition, ongoing process, completed result, or bounded sequence.

example.text: Pisałem raport przez tydzień i wczoraj go napisałem.
example.translation: I wrote the report for a week and finished it yesterday.

name: Relative clauses with który
description: Use który to connect a noun with a relative clause; its case follows its role inside that clause.

example.text: To jest książka, którą dostałem od siostry.
example.translation: This is the book that I received from my sister.

name: Real conditions with jeśli and jeżeli
description: Express likely conditions and consequences with jeśli or jeżeli and ordinary indicative forms.

example.text: Jeśli jutro nie będzie padać, pojedziemy nad jezioro.
example.translation: If it does not rain tomorrow, we will go to the lake.

name: Counterfactual conditions with gdyby
description: Combine gdyby with conditional forms to describe unreal or hypothetical situations.

example.text: Gdybym miał więcej czasu, częściej podróżowałbym.
example.translation: If I had more time, I would travel more often.

name: Correlative clauses with im... tym...
description: Relate two changing quantities or degrees with im and tym.

example.text: Im dłużej czekamy, tym bardziej się niepokoimy.
example.translation: The longer we wait, the more worried we become.

name: Impersonal można, trzeba, należy, warto
description: Use impersonal modal predicates with an infinitive for general possibility, necessity, obligation, or advice.

example.text: Tutaj można odpocząć, ale trzeba zachować ciszę.
example.translation: You can rest here, but you must keep quiet.

name: Common verb and case government
description: Learn the cases selected by frequent verbs rather than translating an English preposition mechanically.

example.text: Pomagam sąsiadowi, słucham muzyki i interesuję się historią.
example.translation: I help my neighbor, listen to music, and am interested in history.

name: Colloquial register
description: Recognize ordinary colloquial choices and keep them separate from neutral and formal usage.

example.text: Daj spokój, zaraz wszystko ci opowiem.
example.translation: Come on, I'll tell you everything in a moment.

name: Comparison and basic text cohesion
description: Link descriptions with comparison, repetition control, synonyms, and clear reference to earlier information.

example.text: Pierwszy plan był prosty, natomiast drugi okazał się bardziej praktyczny.
example.translation: The first plan was simple, whereas the second proved more practical.

name: Irregular verb stems
description: Conjugate verbs with less predictable present, past, imperative, or future stems.

example.text: Niosę ciasto, które wczoraj upiekłem, i zaraz pobiegnę po kawę.
example.translation: I am carrying the cake I baked yesterday, and I will run for coffee shortly.

name: Passive voice
description: Build passive clauses with być or zostać and a passive participle, optionally naming the agent.

example.text: Dokument został podpisany przez dyrektora.
example.translation: The document was signed by the director.

name: Reflexive passive and eventive się
description: Use się constructions when an event is presented without a specific agent.

example.text: Nowy most buduje się bardzo szybko.
example.translation: The new bridge is being built very quickly.

name: Active adjectival participles
description: Form -ący participles from imperfective verbs and decline them like adjectives.

example.text: Studenci czekający przed salą czytali wiadomość.
example.translation: The students waiting outside the room were reading a message.

name: Passive adjectival participles
description: Form and decline passive participles in -ny, -ony, and -ty.

example.text: Wysłana rano paczka została już odebrana.
example.translation: The parcel sent in the morning has already been collected.

name: Contemporary adverbial participles
description: Use -ąc forms for a simultaneous action performed by the same subject.

example.text: Wracając do domu, rozmawiałem przez telefon.
example.translation: While returning home, I talked on the phone.

name: Recognizing anterior adverbial participles
description: Recognize -wszy and -łszy forms that place one completed action before another.

example.text: Przeczytawszy list, schowała go do szuflady.
example.translation: Having read the letter, she put it in the drawer.

name: Impersonal past forms in -no and -to
description: Use -no and -to forms to report a past action without identifying its agent.

example.text: Wczoraj otwarto wystawę i pokazano nowe obrazy.
example.translation: Yesterday the exhibition was opened and new paintings were shown.

name: Impersonal constructions with się
description: Use third-person singular or neuter forms with się for general habits and experiences.

example.text: W tej bibliotece pracuje się spokojnie.
example.translation: One can work calmly in this library.

name: Verbal nouns in -nie and -cie
description: Turn verbs into action nouns while retaining relevant aspect and complements.

example.text: Regularne czytanie prasy ułatwia rozumienie debaty.
example.translation: Regularly reading the press makes understanding debate easier.

name: Agent nouns and feminine counterparts
description: Derive names of people who perform an action and common feminine counterparts.

example.text: Lekarz i lekarka rozmawiali z młodym badaczem.
example.translation: The male and female doctors talked with a young researcher.

name: Place nouns and derived adverbs
description: Derive names of places from verbs and adverbs from adjectives.

example.text: W czytelni można spokojnie czytać po polsku.
example.translation: In the reading room one can read calmly in Polish.

name: Extended indefinite pronouns
description: Distinguish series such as jakiś, któryś, ktokolwiek, cokolwiek, każdy, and żaden.

example.text: Każdy może coś powiedzieć, ale ktokolwiek odpowie, musi podać nazwisko.
example.translation: Everyone may say something, but whoever answers must give a name.

name: Dates and indefinite numerals
description: Decline dates and quantity expressions such as kilka, kilkuset, wiele, and paru.

example.text: Spotkanie kilkuset osób odbyło się dwudziestego maja.
example.translation: A meeting of several hundred people took place on May twentieth.

name: Advanced dative government
description: Use the dative selected by verbs, nouns, and prepositions such as dzięki, wbrew, and przeciwko.

example.text: Dzięki lekarzom pacjent wyzdrowiał wbrew wcześniejszym prognozom.
example.translation: Thanks to the doctors, the patient recovered contrary to earlier forecasts.

name: Expanding and reducing simple sentences
description: Add or remove modifiers while preserving a sentence's core proposition.

example.text: Młodszy brat od rana czyta książkę; krótko mówiąc, brat czyta.
example.translation: The younger brother has been reading a book since morning; in short, the brother is reading.

name: Transforming clauses into phrases
description: Replace a subordinate clause with a nominal, infinitival, prepositional, or participial phrase.

example.text: Namawiał nas, żebyśmy wyjechali wcześniej, czyli na wcześniejszy wyjazd.
example.translation: He urged us to leave earlier, that is, to make an earlier departure.

name: Concessive clauses
description: Use chociaż, choć, mimo że, and pomimo że to contrast an outcome with a potentially opposing fact.

example.text: Mimo że padało, wybraliśmy się na długi spacer.
example.translation: Although it was raining, we went for a long walk.

name: Prepositions inside relative clauses
description: Put a preposition before the correctly declined relative pronoun when its role requires one.

example.text: Człowiek, na którego czekamy, właśnie zadzwonił.
example.translation: The person we are waiting for has just called.

name: Direct and reported speech
description: Report statements, questions, and commands with że, czy, żeby, or an interrogative word.

example.text: Powiedziała, że wróci później, i zapytała, czy będziemy czekać.
example.translation: She said she would return later and asked whether we would wait.

name: Syntactic synonymy
description: Recast the same relation using a clause, participle, nominalization, or different prepositional phrase.

example.text: Wyjechali z powodu burzy, czyli dlatego, że nadchodziła burza.
example.translation: They left because of the storm, that is, because a storm was approaching.

name: Multi-clause sentence structure
description: Organize several coordinated and subordinated clauses without losing their logical relationships.

example.text: Kiedy wrócił, otworzył okno, ale nie zauważył, że zaczęło padać.
example.translation: When he returned, he opened the window but did not notice that it had started raining.

name: Real, possible, and unreal conditionals
description: Distinguish factual conditions, open hypothetical conditions, and counterfactual conditions.

example.text: Jeśli zdążę, przyjdę; gdybym nie zdążył, zadzwoniłbym.
example.translation: If I make it, I will come; if I did not, I would call.

name: Productive derivation and compounds
description: Build transparent action, person, place, adjective, and compound forms from familiar bases.

example.text: Bezalkoholowy napój przygotował pracownik trzypokojowego hotelu.
example.translation: A worker at the three-room hotel prepared a nonalcoholic drink.

name: Diminutives, endearments, and augmentatives
description: Recognize how derivational suffixes add size, affection, irony, or negative evaluation.

example.text: Dziecko dostało książeczkę, a nie ciężkie tomisko.
example.translation: The child received a little book, not a heavy tome.

name: Collective numerals
description: Use collective numerals with mixed-gender groups, young beings, and plural-only nouns.

example.text: Troje dzieci znalazło dwoje drzwi do starego archiwum.
example.translation: Three children found two doors to the old archive.

name: Fractional numerals and long number strings
description: Decline fractions and select agreement within long, multi-part numeral expressions.

example.text: Brakuje nam trzech czwartych z dwóch tysięcy pięciuset złotych.
example.translation: We are missing three quarters of two thousand five hundred złoty.

name: Polish and foreign name declension
description: Decline Polish and foreign first names and surnames according to their form and bearer.

example.text: Rozmawialiśmy o powieściach Sienkiewicza i reportażach Kapuścińskiego.
example.translation: We discussed Sienkiewicz's novels and Kapuściński's reportage.

name: Complex nominal groups and titles
description: Coordinate agreement and declension inside names with titles, professions, and apposition.

example.text: Spotkaliśmy się z panią profesor Ewą Nowak.
example.translation: We met Professor Ewa Nowak.

name: Limits on declining names and titles
description: Recognize contemporary contexts where a title or name remains unchanged and avoid forced inflection.

example.text: Decyzję omówiono z minister edukacji Anną Nowak.
example.translation: The decision was discussed with Education Minister Anna Nowak.

name: Verb prefixes and lexical meaning
description: Interpret how prefixes add path, completion, repetition, intensity, distribution, or other meanings beyond aspect.

example.text: Autor dopisał zakończenie, przepisał rozdział i rozpisał plan.
example.translation: The author added an ending, rewrote the chapter, and laid out a plan.

name: Anterior adverbial participles
description: Form and use -wszy or -łszy participles for a completed action sharing the main clause's subject.

example.text: Przeczytawszy raport, minister odpowiedział na pytania.
example.translation: Having read the report, the minister answered the questions.

name: Managing agency with passive and impersonal forms
description: Choose passive voice, się, or -no and -to according to whether the agent is known, relevant, or deliberately suppressed.

example.text: Ustalono, że wniosek zostanie rozpatrzony po uzupełnieniu dokumentów.
example.translation: It was established that the application would be considered after the documents were completed.

name: Advanced nominal and instrumental derivation
description: Derive abstract qualities, events, agents, places, and instruments while handling stem changes.

example.text: Ostrzeżenie kierowcy zapobiegło zderzeniu, a spinacz uratował dokumenty.
example.translation: The driver's warning prevented a collision, and a paper clip saved the documents.

name: Nominalization and sentence equivalents
description: Compress clauses into nominalizations, participial phrases, and sentence equivalents appropriate to formal prose.

example.text: Po zakończeniu konsultacji przystąpiono do sporządzenia raportu.
example.translation: After the consultations ended, preparation of the report began.

name: Advanced clause connectors
description: Use connectors such as o ile, jak gdyby, dopóki, aniżeli, mimo to, and wobec tego precisely.

example.text: O ile komisja wyrazi zgodę, projekt ruszy, wobec tego czekamy.
example.translation: Provided the committee agrees, the project will start; therefore, we are waiting.

name: Official, academic, journalistic, and artistic registers
description: Select characteristic vocabulary and syntax for official, academic, journalistic, and literary contexts.

example.text: Przeprowadzenie analizy umożliwiło komisji sformułowanie zaleceń.
example.translation: Conducting the analysis enabled the committee to formulate recommendations.

name: Rhetorical composition and cohesion
description: Structure a sustained argument with transitions, controlled emphasis, parallelism, and a clear progression.

example.text: Najpierw określmy problem, następnie oceńmy dane, a na końcu przedstawmy wnioski.
example.translation: First let us define the problem, then assess the data, and finally present the conclusions.

name: Core figurative and rhetorical devices
description: Recognize and use metaphor, rhetorical questions, archaism, dialect, sound imitation, and evaluative word formation.

example.text: Czy naprawdę morze świateł mogło zasnąć przed świtem?
example.translation: Could the sea of lights really fall asleep before dawn?

name: Common-gender and collective nouns
description: Control agreement with rare common-gender, collective, and expressive human nouns.

example.text: To sierota był później znanym artystą, a hrabiostwo zostali mecenasami.
example.translation: That orphan later became a famous artist, and the count and countess became patrons.

name: Variant plurals with different meanings
description: Distinguish nouns whose alternative plural forms carry different lexical meanings.

example.text: Organy państwa sprawdziły organa w zabytkowym kościele.
example.translation: State authorities inspected the organs in the historic church.

name: Rare and expressive declension patterns
description: Decline rare, regional, archaic, expressive, and phonologically difficult noun patterns.

example.text: Po gołoledzi ślady kuropatwy prowadziły w stronę cerkwi.
example.translation: After the black ice, the partridge's tracks led toward the Orthodox church.

name: Rare and difficult verb conjugation
description: Produce infrequent and irregular forms whose stems undergo substantial alternation.

example.text: Choć łgał i plótł bzdury, tłum wciąż do niego lgnął.
example.translation: Although he lied and talked nonsense, the crowd still clung to him.

name: Fine-grained prefix meaning
description: Interpret subtle lexical effects of prefixes, including reversal, separation, restoration, removal, and ceremonial completion.

example.text: Odbarwił tkaninę, odrysował wzór i odśpiewał hymn.
example.translation: He bleached the fabric, traced the pattern, and sang the anthem ceremonially.

name: Creative compounds and word formation
description: Interpret and create structurally possible compounds and derivatives while controlling their register and connotation.

example.text: Niebosiężny wieżowiec stał się symbolem wielkomiejskiego pośpiechu.
example.translation: The sky-high skyscraper became a symbol of metropolitan haste.

name: Advanced syntactic transformations
description: Move fluently among clauses, nominal phrases, participial constructions, ellipsis, and complex sentence equivalents.

example.text: Dotarłszy na skraj wsi, ujrzał pola, które ciągnęły się po horyzont.
example.translation: Having reached the edge of the village, he saw fields stretching to the horizon.

name: Marked word order and information focus
description: Exploit Polish word-order freedom to stage given and new information or create contrast without ambiguity.

example.text: Nie sam wynik ich zaskoczył, lecz łatwość jego osiągnięcia.
example.translation: It was not the result itself that surprised them, but the ease of achieving it.

name: Idiomatic and formal case government
description: Control less predictable case patterns selected by formal verbs, adjectives, nouns, and fixed expressions.

example.text: Decyzja nie pozostaje bez wpływu na sposób podlegania ocenie.
example.translation: The decision is not without influence on the manner of being assessed.

name: Stylistic variation and reformulation
description: Recast propositions across neutral, formal, colloquial, ironic, and literary registers using a broad range of stylistic devices.

example.text: To, co w raporcie nazwano korektą, pracownicy określili po prostu jako chaos.
example.translation: What the report called an adjustment, employees simply described as chaos.
